purpose: Re-run 1.5B GPU-only + Hybrid benchmarks after silencing per-request/per-call stdout (hybrid_core, core_client, cpu_worker, cpu_attn, serve.sh). Compare vs previous 20260411_060412/060712 runs from the same dev host to see if the logging reduction is measurable.

stdout_line_count.notes: Previous run had VLLM_HYBRID_TRACE_EVERY=500 via serve.sh default (now 0). Previous server log included ~130 [HYBRID-CPU-ATTN] + [HYBRID-CPU-EXEC] + Request finished per-req lines. New log has only upstream uvicorn access + vllm engine stats during serving.

conclusion.gpu_only_is_change_significant: No meaningful perf difference. TP +1.7%, TTFT -4.6%, TPOT unchanged. Within normal dev run-to-run variance (~2-5%). Note GPU util 50% -> 65% and power +16% suggest the GPU is fed more aggressively now, but this may reflect the reduced stdout blocking or simply variance — wall time identical (14.30 vs 14.31 s).
conclusion.hybrid_is_change_significant: No. All metrics within 1% of prev run. TPOT -0.4%, wall identical. The hybrid CPU-bound tail (CPU 2 reqs × 13s latency) dominates wall time; any tiny stdout-reduction benefit is invisible against the 17s duration.
conclusion.why_dev_shows_small_benefit: dev previously had TRACE_EVERY=500 which emits ~450 log lines/sec during an 8s GPU-only burst. The OS stdout pipe absorbs this without blocking. The dramatic stdout-blocking failure was observed on H100x4 with TRACE=1 (every call, ~120000 lines/sec) where the API server main thread serialized — that is where the fix will matter most.
conclusion.tpot_evidence: TPOT (per-token step time) is the sensitive indicator of mid-serving stdout blocking. GPU-only TPOT: 27.79 -> 27.79 (identical). Hybrid TPOT: 30.05 -> 29.92 (-0.4%). No mid-serving stdout bottleneck on dev for this workload — confirming the stdout-reduction was a correctness / production-hygiene fix rather than a perf fix on dev.
conclusion.stdout_reduction_is_real: Server log line count for hybrid dropped from 2701 to 1094 (-59.5%). All HYBRID per-req/per-call markers are zero during serving. Boot markers preserved (11). Behavior matches intended design.
